channel.title: 2026-05-22 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-05-22 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: 光子损耗收支平衡点的无源量子纠错
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. X
👤 Shruti Shirol, Sean van Geldern, Hanzhe Xi, and Chen Wang
研究人员在“收支平衡”点实现了无源量子纠错。通过使用连续驱动而非主动测量，他们成功地将量子比特的寿命延长至超出其物理极限。这项工作为实现更稳定和长寿命的量子信息处理提供了重要进展。

item.title: Discrete Fourier-transform-based quantum circuit for modular multiplication in Shor's algorithm
item.description:
📚 Editors' Suggestions
👤 Abu Musa Patoary, Amit Vikram, and Victor Galitski
Author(s): Abu Musa Patoary, Amit Vikram, and Victor Galitski The modular exponentiation operation has long limited the practical applications of Shor’s algorithm, and has often had to be tailored to the number being factorized. Here, the authors present a modular exponentiation circuit using generalized discrete Fourier transforms, achieving a desirable gate complexity. [Phys. Rev. A 113, 052448] Published Fri May 22, 2026

item.title: Quantum supremacy just ran into an unexpected rival: An ordinary laptop armed with new math
item.description:
📚 Physics News
Using a conventional computer and cutting-edge mathematical tools and code, physicists at the Center for Computational Quantum Physics (CCQ) at the Simons Foundation's Flatiron Institute and collaborators at Boston University have cracked a daunting quantum physics problem previously claimed to be solvable only by quantum computers.

item.title: 量子多体系统中纯度的张量交叉插值
item.description:
📚 Quantum
👤 Dmytro Kolisnyk, Raimel A. Medina, Romain Vasseur, and Maksym Serbyn
量子多体系统希尔伯特空间的指数级增长使得完整的态表征极具挑战。最近提出的纠缠特征提供了一种紧凑存储子区域纯度的方法。本研究利用张量交叉插值（TCI）算法，证明了在纠缠特征可表示为有限键维MPS的假设下，可以通过多项式数量的样本高效学习纠缠特征。该学习过程在Haar态和随机MPS态上得到了基准测试验证，并与分析预期相符。

item.title: [ASAP] Many-Body Exciton Interactions, Coherence, and Transport in Perovskite Quantum Dots
item.description:
📚 Nano Letters: Latest Articles (ACS Publications)
👤 Kinjol Barua, Sonja G. Nusser, Shriya Gumber, Nicholas R. Favate, Emma Daggett, Meng-Jia Sun, Jonathan D. Hood, Jianshu Cao, Christina W. Li, Oleg V. Prezhdo, and Libai Huang
Nano Letters DOI: 10.1021/acs.nanolett.6c01029

item.title: Erratum: Quantum simulation with gauge fixing: From Ising lattice gauge theory to dynamical flux model [Phys. Rev. Research 7 , 013311 (2025)]
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Junsen Wang, Xiangxiang Sun, and Wei Zheng
Author(s): Junsen Wang, Xiangxiang Sun, and Wei Zheng [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 029001] Published Fri May 22, 2026

item.title: Matrix Product Operator Encodings of the Magnus Expansion and Dyson Series
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Victor Vanthilt, Maarten Van Damme, Jutho Haegeman, Ian P. McCulloch, Laurens Vanderstraeten
arXiv:2605.21597v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We introduce a matrix product operator (MPO) encoding of the Magnus expansion and the Dyson series for one-dimensional quantum lattice models with time-dependent Hamiltonians. The MPO construction can be made accurate up to arbitrary order in the time step, it can be applied to both finite and infinite systems, and it can handle long-range interactions. The resulting MPO can be combined with state-of-the-art time evolution algorithms based on matrix product states, allowing for drastic improvements in simulating evolution under time-dependent Hamiltonians. Our MPO construction can also be used for the optimization of quantum circuits in the context of quantum simulation of time-dependent Hamiltonians.

item.title: Reduced Dynamical Maps in Finite Temperature Vibronic Coupling Models via Choi Matrices: Numerical Methods and Applications
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Raffaele Borrelli, Hideaki Takahashi
arXiv:2605.22459v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We present a streamlined implementation of a computational framework for constructing and analyzing reduced dynamical maps for complex system--bath models at finite temperature. The methodology is based on three established ingredients of quantum dynamics: the Choi--Jamiołkowski isomorphism for the representation of quantum channels, thermofield (TFD) purification of thermal environments, and tensor-train (TT) propagation of the resulting enlarged pure state. The reduced map is obtained from a single unitary propagation in a thermofield-doubled Hilbert space and represented in matrix form through the Choi--Jamiołkowski isomorphism. The TFD evolution is implemented in the TT representation, enabling efficient propagation of high-dimensional…

item.title: Complexity of Quantum Trajectories
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Luca Lumia, Emanuele Tirrito, Mario Collura, Fabian H. L. Essler, Rosario Fazio
arXiv:2602.00232v3 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Open quantum systems can be described by unraveling Lindblad master equations into ensembles of quantum trajectories. Here we investigate how the complexity of such trajectories is affected by conservation laws and other dynamical constraints of the underlying Lindblad evolution. We characterize this complexity using a data-driven approach based on the intrinsic dimension, defined as the minimal number of variables required to encode the information contained in a data set. Applying this framework to several systems, including dissipative variants of the quantum top and of the XXZ chain, we find that the intrinsic dimension is sensitive to the structure of their dynamics. The Lindblad evolution in these systems is typically chaotic…

item.title: Disorder-Free Localization and Fragmentation in a Non-Abelian Lattice Gauge Theory
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Giovanni Cataldi, Giuseppe Calajó, Pietro Silvi, Simone Montangero, Jad C. Halimeh
arXiv:2505.04704v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We investigate how isolated quantum many-body systems dynamically equilibrate under non-Abelian gauge-symmetry constraints. By encoding gauge superselection sectors into static SU(2) background charges, we map out the dynamical phase diagram of a (1+1)D SU(2) lattice gauge theory with dynamical matter. We uncover three distinct regimes: (i) an ergodic phase, (ii) a fragmented phase that is nonthermal but delocalized, and (iii) a disorder-free many-body localized regime. In the latter, a superposition of gauge superselection sectors preserves spatial matter inhomogeneities in time, as evidenced by distinct temporal scalings of entropy. We highlight the non-Abelian nature of these phases and argue for potential realizations on qudit proces…

item.title: [ASAP] Anomalous Dispersion via Dissipative Coupling in a Quantum Well Exciton-Polariton Microcavity
item.description:
📚 Nano Letters: Latest Articles (ACS Publications)
👤 Dąbrówka Biegańska, Maciej Pieczarka, Christian Schneider, Sven Höfling, Sebastian Klembt, and Marcin Syperek
Nano Letters DOI: 10.1021/acs.nanolett.6c00554

item.title: The Möbius Ring Topology of Dimensional Space: A Topological Resolution of the Black Hole Information Paradox
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Hao Huang
The black hole information paradox stands as the longest-standing unresolved conflict between general relativity and quantum mechanics. Here we propose a resolution rooted in the Möbius-ring topology of dimensional space, derived from an axiomatic field framework grounded in Einstein's relativistic postulates. The dimension parameter α obeys the master equation α = ln 2 + (1 − ln 2)[Q(t) − C(t)], with Q(t) and C(t) the quantum and classical (entropy) response fields. Three critical constants—α₀ = ln 2, β₁ = 1 − ln 2, and Rc = (2 ln 2 − 1)/(ln 2)² ≈ 0.804—are derived analytically from hyperbolic geometry and Lorentz-invariant dissipative systems via five exact theorems, establishing a deductive bridge from cosmic-scale physics to topological order parameters. The bridge mapping Φ(r) establ…

item.title: Theory-Guided, Machine-Learning-Accelerated Discovery of a 3D Carbon Nested Nodal-Surface Semimetal
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Shuaihua Zhang, Silei Guo, Jingxiang Liu, Baoxin Hu, Yanling Wu, Jun Li
arXiv:2605.22141v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Extending the Dirac physics of two-dimensional (2D) graphene into three dimensions (3D) carbon allotropes with higher-dimensional band degeneracies remains a central challenge in topological materials science. Here, we propose a general symmetry-engineering principle that systematically transforms graphene's Dirac cone into a 3D nodal surface via controlled layering and registry shift, and employ this principle to guide a machine-learning-accelerated inverse design. By integrating a crystal diffusion variational autoencoder(CDVAE) with a Crystal Transformer, we discover a novel, dynamically and mechanically stable carbon allotrope named Netsene (bct-C₂₄), which crystallizes in the body-centered tetragonal I4/mcm space group. First-principles…

item.title: Upscaling DFT-trained machine-learning interatomic potential toward Quantum Monte Carlo accuracy: Sulfur-vacancy migration in monolayer MoS₂ as a testbed
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Adam Hložný, Ján Brndiar, Ye Luo, Ivan Štich
arXiv:2605.22601v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We designed a procedure to train a machine learning interatomic potential (MLIP) at benchmark-quality quantum Monte Carlo (QMC) accuracy. To avoid the complexities of high-quality atomic force determination with the stochastic QMC methods, we use a multi-fidelity approach wherein high-level QMC energies are used alongside suitably processed low-level DFT atomic forces to train a QMC fine-tuned MLIP which significantly improves both the energetics and atomic forces over the baseline DFT-based MLIP. Fine-tuning is only applied to the readout layers of an equivariant message-passing MACE MLIP. We used sulfur mono- and multiple vacancies in monolayer MoS₂ as a testbed and demonstrate a near QMC accuracy of the model in a number of in- and out-of…

item.title: Computing band gaps of periodic materials via sample-based quantum diagonalization

item.title: Mesoscale hydrogen-bond network engineering controls quantum-coherent proton transport to suppress aluminum corrosion

📚 AAAS: Science Advances: Table of Contents

item.title: Multi‐Self‐Trapped‐Exciton Engineering in Sb‐Doped Zr‐Hybrid‐Halides Towards Near‐Unity Quantum Efficiency and Excitation‐Tunable Luminescence
item.description:
📚 Wiley
👤 Luyao Wei, Shilin Jin, Wanxin Shi, Zhenghuan Lin, Yuehua Chen, Lingwei Zeng, Daqin Chen
Advanced Functional Materials, EarlyView.

item.title: Neural canonical transformation for the spectra of fluxional molecule CH+ 5
item.description:
📚 Machine Learning: Science and Technology
👤 Ruisi Wang, Qi Zhang and Lei Wang
Protonated methane, , is a highly fluxional molecule with large spatial motions of the hydrogen atoms. The molecule’s anharmonic effects and the delocalized wavefunction of the hydrogen atoms significantly affect the excitation spectrum of the molecule. The neural canonical transformation (NCT) approach, which we previously developed to solve the vibrational spectra of molecules and solids, is a powerful method that effectively treats nuclear quantum effects and anharmonicities. Using NCT with wavefunctions in atomic coordinates rather than normal coordinates, we successfully calculate the ground and excited states of . We found that the wavefunctions for the ground state, as well as for low- and high-energy excited states, show preferences for the three stationary points on the potential…

item.title: Quantum interference in ring-structured molecular junctions: Effects of electron–phonon coupling and lattice dynamics
item.description:
📚 The Journal of Chemical Physics Current Issue
We investigate the quantum interference (QI) effects in molecular junctions with ring geometry, focusing on the role of electron–phonon (e–ph) coupling and lattice dynamics, using the extended Su–Schrieffer–Heeger model combined with the hierarchical equations of motion approach. In an ideal uniform lattice without e–ph coupling, the current exhibits a clear even–odd dependence on the atom number difference Δ between the two branches, with constructive quantum interference (CQI) for even Δ/2 and destructive quantum interference (DQI) for odd Δ/2. Under frozen-lattice conditions, slight lattice reconstruction induced by the e–ph coupling weakens both CQI and DQI by modifying the phase accumulation along the transport pathways. In contrast, dynamical lattice evolution leads to a pronounced…

item.title: Recent Advances of Bulk Photovoltaic Effect in Exotic Quantum Materials: Progress and Challenges

item.title: Spin glass in quantum paraelectric KTaO3: Supercritical CO2-induced monoclinic phase
item.description:
📚 AAAS: Science Advances: Table of Contents
👤 Yuning Liang and Qun Xu
Science Advances, Volume 12, Issue 21, May 2026.

item.title: Tea-Derived Carbon Quantum Dot-Modified PCN-415-NH2 Nanocomposite as a Bifunctional Adsorbent–Photocatalyst for Efficient Wastewater Treatment under Visible Light
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Tina Reyhani, Azita Shahnazi, Ali Haseli, Roya Sedghi
The growing concern over water contamination by industrial dyes underscores the urgent need for efficient photocatalytic materials. In this study, a novel nanocomposite, PCN-415-NH2/CQDs, was synthesized by incorporating Carbon quantum dots (CQDs) sourced from black tea into a Ti-Zr-based metal-organic framework (MOF), PCN-415-NH2. The resulting PCN-415-NH2/CQDs functioned as an efficient adsorbent and a visible-light-responsive photocatalyst for the degradation of rhodamine B (RhB), demonstrating enhanced performance through synergistic adsorption and photocatalytic mechanisms. Comprehensive characterization via ATR, XRD, FE-SEM, TEM, EDX, BET, and DRS confirmed the successful incorporation of CQDs without disrupting the MOF’s crystalline integrity. Under visible light irradiation, the n…

item.title: Vibrational energy transfer of water at interfaces mediated by Fermi resonance
item.description:
📚 The Journal of Chemical Physics Current Issue
Combining neural network-based molecular dynamics simulations with the mixed quantum/classical approach, we elucidate the vibrational energy transfer pathways of interfacial OH groups (3115–3285 cm −1 ). This study reveals that intermolecular transfer exhibits the slowest kinetics due to the attenuated dipole–dipole coupling at interfaces, while reorientation, intramolecular, and Fermi-resonant pathways proceed faster. Notably, Fermi resonance largely influences the observed energy transfer dynamics. The overall time is in agreement with experimental measurements. These findings establish Fermi resonance as a critical factor in the vibrational energy transfer near 3200 cm −1 at the gas/water interface.
